title: convolvulus sanguineus
intro: Convolvulus sanguineus, also known as crimson glory vine or crimson bindweed, is a flowering vine native to Australia and New Zealand. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Convolvulus sanguineus include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Herbal preparations of Convolvulus sanguineus are typically made from the leaves and flowers, and can be consumed as a tea, tincture, or applied topically as a salve.
